PYACS
  • pyacs.lib.astrotime package
  • View page source

pyacs.lib.astrotime package

Submodules

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2datetime module

Convert a calendar date to a Python datetime instance.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2datetime.cal2datetime(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s). At 12:00 (ut=0.5) by default.

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2dayno module

Return the day of year (doy).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2dayno.cal2dayno(day, month, year)[source]

Return the day of year (doy).

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

Returns:

Day of year.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(1,1,2000)
>>> 1
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29,2,2000)
>>> 60
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29.,2,2000)
>>> 60.
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29,2,2001)
>>> !!!  29  out of range
>>> !!! bad day

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2decyear module

Convert a calendar date to decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2decyear.cal2decyear(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date to decimal year.

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2mjd module

Convert a calendar date (universal time) to modified Julian day (JD-2400000.5).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2mjd.cal2mjd(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date (universal time) to modified Julian day (JD-2400000.5).

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2mjd(29,2,2000)
>>> 51603.5
>>> pyacs.cal2mjd(29,2,2000,ut=0.0)
>>> 51603.0

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2cal module

Convert Python datetime to calendar date.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2cal.datetime2cal(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to calendar date.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • mday (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2dayno module

Convert Python datetime to day of year, year, and day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2dayno.datetime2dayno(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to day of year, year, and day fraction.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • dayno (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2decyear module

Convert Python datetime to decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2decyear.datetime2decyear(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to decimal year.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2mjd module

Convert Python datetime to modified Julian day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2mjd.datetime2mjd(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2seconds module

Convert datetime to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2seconds.datetime2seconds(np_datetime)[source]

Convert datetime to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Parameters:

np_datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

1D array of seconds (int64).

Return type:

ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_from_calarray module

Build a datetime array from an array of calendar dates.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_from_calarray.datetime_from_calarray(calarray, hour=12, minute=0, sec=0, ms=0)[source]

Build a datetime array from an array of calendar dates.

Parameters:

  • calarray (array-like, 1D or 2D) – Columns order: year, month, mday, hour, minute, second, microsecond.

  • hour (int, optional) – Default hour if not in calarray (default 12).

  • minute (int, optional) – Default minute (default 0).

  • sec (int, optional) – Default second (default 0).

  • ms (int, optional) – Default millisecond (default 0).

Returns:

1D array of datetime instances.

Return type:

ndarray

Notes

year, month, mday are mandatory. If hour/minute/sec/ms not provided, defaults are hour=12, minute=0, sec=0, ms=0 (middle of day).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_round_second module

Round a Python datetime instance to the nearest second.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_round_second.datetime_round_second(my_datetime)[source]

Round a Python datetime instance to the nearest second.

Parameters:

my_datetime (datetime) – Datetime instance to round.

Returns:

New rounded datetime (microsecond set to 0).

Return type:

datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.day_since_decyear module

Calculate the number of days since a reference decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.day_since_decyear.day_since_decyear(decyear, ref_decyear)[source]

Calculate the number of days since a reference decimal year.

Parameters:

  • decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year(s).

  • ref_decyear (float) – Reference decimal year.

Returns:

Days elapsed. Negative means before reference date. Useful for postseismic analysis.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> ref_decyear=pyacs.cal2decyear(16,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33))
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(17,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 32)),ref_decyear)
>>> 0.9999884259232203
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(17,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33)),ref_decyear)
>>> 0.9999999999854481
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(16,4,2017,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33)),ref_decyear)
>>> 364.9999999999345
>>> ld=pyacs.mjd2decyear(np.arange(pyacs.cal2mjd(13,4,2016),pyacs.cal2mjd(19,4,2016)))
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(ld,ref_decyear)
>>> array([-3.499, -2.499, -1.499, -0.499,  0.501,  1.501])

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2cal module

Return the day and month corresponding to day of year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2cal.dayno2cal(dayno, year)[source]

Return the day and month corresponding to day of year.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year (1-366).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

Returns:

  • day (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2datetime module

Convert day of year and year to a Python datetime instance.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2datetime.dayno2datetime(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (12:00).

Returns:

Datetime instance(s). At 12:00 by default; specify ut for other times.

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2decyear module

Convert day of year and year to decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2decyear.dayno2decyear(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to decimal year.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (12:00).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

Accounts for leap years (366 days). Unless ut is specified, the result is at 12:00 (ut=0.5).

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,1999)
>>> 1999.16301369863
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2000)
>>> 2000.1625683060108
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2001)
>>> 2001.16301369863
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2001,ut=0.)
>>> 2001.1616438356164

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2mjd module

Convert day of year and year to modified Julian day (JD - 2400000.5).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2mjd.dayno2mjd(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to modified Julian day (JD - 2400000.5).

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.dayno2mjd(60,2000)
>>> 51603.5
>>> pyacs.dayno2mjd(60.,2000,ut=0)
>>> 51603.0

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2cal module

Convert decimal year to calendar date.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2cal.decyear2cal(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to calendar date.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

  • mday (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2datetime module

Convert decimal year to a Python datetime instance.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2datetime.decyear2datetime(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2dayno module

Convert decimal year to day of year and day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2dayno.decyear2dayno(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to day of year and day fraction.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

  • noday (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2epoch module

Convert decimal year to formatted epoch string ‘yy:doy:sec’ (%02d:%03d:%05d).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2epoch.decyear2epoch(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to formatted epoch string ‘yy:doy:sec’ (%02d:%03d:%05d).

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Epoch string(s) ‘yy:doy:sec’.

Return type:

str or ndarray

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2decyear(1,3,2001)
>>> 2001.16301369863
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(2001.16301369863)
>>> '01:060:43200'
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(2000.16301369863)
>>> '00:060:57284'
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(pyacs.cal2decyear(1,3,2000))
>>> '00:061:43200'

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2mjd module

Convert decimal year to modified Julian day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2mjd.decyear2mjd(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2seconds module

Convert decimal year to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2seconds.decyear2seconds(np_decyear, rounding='day')[source]

Convert decimal year to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Parameters:

  • np_decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year(s).

  • rounding ({'day', 'hour', 'minute', 'second'}, optional) – Rounding: ‘day’ at 12:00:00, ‘hour’ at 00:00, ‘minute’ at current minute with 00 seconds, ‘second’ at integer second.

Returns:

1D integer (np.int64) array of seconds.

Return type:

ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.epoch2decyear module

Convert SINEX-format epoch string ‘YY:DOY:SOD’ (e.g. ‘17:100:43185’) to decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.epoch2decyear.epoch2decyear(epoch)[source]

Convert SINEX-format epoch string ‘YY:DOY:SOD’ (e.g. ‘17:100:43185’) to decimal year.

Parameters:

epoch (str or array-like) – Epoch string(s).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.gpsweek2mjd module

Convert GPS week and day of week to modified Julian day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.gpsweek2mjd.gpsweek2mjd(gpsweek, dow)[source]

Convert GPS week and day of week to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

  • gpsweek (int or array-like) – GPS week number.

  • dow (int or array-like) – Day of week (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.guess_date module

Guess a date from input (string or float) and return decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.guess_date.guess_date(date)[source]

Guess a date from input (string or float) and return decimal year.

Parameters:

date (str or float) – Date as string (e.g. ‘2016/04/16’, ‘2016 107’, ‘57500.0’) or float (MJD or decimal year).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float

Examples

Calendar date formats (year/month/day or day/month/year): >>> import pyacs >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016/04/16’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016-04-16’) 2016.2909836065573

Day of year format (year doy or doy year): >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016 107’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘107 2016’) 2016.2909836065573

Decimal year format: >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016.29’) 2016.29 >>> pyacs.guess_date(2016.29) 2016.29

MJD (Modified Julian Date) format: >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘57500.0’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(57500.0) 2016.2909836065573

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2ut module

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to fractional day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2ut.hmsmicros2ut(h, mn, s=0, microsecond=0)[source]

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to fractional day.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Hours.

  • mn (int) – Minutes.

  • s (int, optional) – Seconds (default 0).

  • microsecond (int or float, optional) – Microseconds (default 0).

Returns:

Fractional day (ut) in [0., 1.[.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2uts module

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2uts.hmsmicros2uts(h, mn, s=0, microsecond=0)[source]

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Hours.

  • mn (int) – Minutes.

  • s (int, optional) – Seconds (default 0).

  • microsecond (int or float, optional) – Microseconds (default 0).

Returns:

Seconds since 00:00:00.0 (uts).

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jd2mjd module

Convert Julian day to modified Julian day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jd2mjd.jd2mjd(jd)[source]

Convert Julian day to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

jd (float) – Julian day.

Returns:

jd - 2400000.5.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_datetime module

Convert JPL date to datetime object (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_datetime.jpldate_2_datetime(jpldate)[source]

Convert JPL date to datetime object.

JPL dates are seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 (TAI).

Parameters:

jpldate (float or array-like) – JPL date (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_decyear module

Convert JPL date to decimal year (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_decyear.jpldate_2_decyear(jpldate)[source]

Convert JPL date to decimal year.

JPL dates are seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 (TAI).

Parameters:

jpldate (float or array-like) – JPL date (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.leap_year module

Return True if year is a leap year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.leap_year.leap_year(year)[source]

Return True if year is a leap year.

Parameters:

year (int or array-like) – Year (YYYY).

Returns:

True if leap year, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2cal module

Convert modified Julian day to calendar date (universal time).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2cal.mjd2cal(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to calendar date (universal time).

Based on the slalib routine sla_djcl.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day (JD - 2400000.5).

Returns:

  • day (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2datetime module

Convert modified Julian day to a Python datetime instance.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2datetime.mjd2datetime(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2dayno module

Convert modified Julian day to year and day of year (universal time).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2dayno.mjd2dayno(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to year and day of year (universal time).

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

  • dayno (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.mjd2dayno(51603.0)
>>> (60, 2000, 0.0)
>>> pyacs.mjd2dayno(51603.5)
>>> (60, 2000, 0.5)

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2decyear module

Convert modified Julian day to decimal year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2decyear.mjd2decyear(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to decimal year.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2gpsweek module

Convert modified Julian day to GPS week and GPS day of week.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2gpsweek.mjd2gpsweek(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to GPS week and GPS day of week.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

  • gps_week (int or ndarray) – GPS week number.

  • day_of_week (int or ndarray) – Day of week (0-6).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2jd module

Convert modified Julian day to Julian day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2jd.mjd2jd(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to Julian day.

Parameters:

mjd (float) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

mjd + 2400000.5.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.seconds2datetime module

Convert seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00 to datetime object(s).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.seconds2datetime.seconds2datetime(seconds)[source]

Convert seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00 to datetime object(s).

Parameters:

seconds (int or array-like) – Seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.ut2uts module

Convert fractional day to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.ut2uts.ut2uts(ut)[source]

Convert fractional day to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

Parameters:

ut (float) – Fractional day in [0., 1.[.

Returns:

ut * 60.*60.*24. (seconds in day).

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2hmsmicros module

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 (0-86400) to hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2hmsmicros.uts2hmsmicros(uts)[source]

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 (0-86400) to hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds.

Parameters:

uts (float or array-like) – Seconds since 00:00:00.0, in [0., 86400.[.

Returns:

  • h (int or ndarray) – Hours.

  • mn (int or ndarray) – Minutes.

  • s (int or ndarray) – Seconds.

  • microsecond (float or ndarray) – Microseconds.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.uts2hmsmicros(86399.999999)
>>> (23, 59, 59, 999999.0000069374)
>>> pyacs.uts2hmsmicros(86400)
>>> !!!  86400 uts out of range [0- 86400.0 [

pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2ut module

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 to fractional day.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2ut.uts2ut(uts)[source]

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 to fractional day.

Parameters:

uts (float or array-like) – Seconds in [0., 86400.[.

Returns:

uts / (60.*60.*24.) (fractional day).

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.year2yr module

Convert 4-digit year to 2-digit year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.year2yr.year2yr(year)[source]

Convert 4-digit year to 2-digit year.

Parameters:

year (int or array-like) – 4-digit year (e.g. 2001).

Returns:

2-digit year (e.g. 01). year-2000 if year>1999, else year-1900.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Notes

Heritage from geodesy convention (GPS in 1980). See examples.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.year2yr(1996)
>>> 96
>>> pyacs.year2yr(2011)
>>> 11

pyacs.lib.astrotime.yr2year module

Convert two-digit year to four-digit year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.yr2year.yr2year(yr)[source]

Convert two-digit year to four-digit year.

Parameters:

yr (int or array-like) – 2-digit year (e.g. 01).

Returns:

4-digit year (e.g. 2001). yr+2000 if yr<80, else yr+1900.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Notes

Heritage from geodesy convention (GPS era). See examples.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.yr2year(96)
>>> 1996
>>> pyacs.yr2year(11)
>>> 2011

Module contents

AstroTime: time conversions (calendar, MJD, decimal year, datetime).

Traduction from perl module Astro::Time (CPAN, Chris Phillips). Conversion to Python by Jean-Mathieu Nocquet (Geoazur). Routines for calendar dates, Modified Julian day, UT/local sidereal time, and numerical/string angles. String functions removed. Datetime support added.

Notes

This module is for date manipulation; leap seconds are not handled. See the GPSTime module for GPS/UTC conversions. In Python 3.6+, / was changed to // for integer operations. All conversions not specifying ut or uts use 12h00mn00s as default; ut is the decimal fraction of day, uts is seconds since 00h00m00.0s.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2datetime(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s). At 12:00 (ut=0.5) by default.

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2dayno(day, month, year)[source]

Return the day of year (doy).

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

Returns:

Day of year.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(1,1,2000)
>>> 1
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29,2,2000)
>>> 60
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29.,2,2000)
>>> 60.
>>> pyacs.cal2dayno(29,2,2001)
>>> !!!  29  out of range
>>> !!! bad day
pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2decyear(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date to decimal year.

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.cal2mjd(day, month, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert a calendar date (universal time) to modified Julian day (JD-2400000.5).

Parameters:

  • day (int or array-like) – Day of month.

  • month (int or array-like) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2mjd(29,2,2000)
>>> 51603.5
>>> pyacs.cal2mjd(29,2,2000,ut=0.0)
>>> 51603.0
pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2cal(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to calendar date.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • mday (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2dayno(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to day of year, year, and day fraction.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • dayno (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2decyear(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to decimal year.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2mjd(datetime)[source]

Convert Python datetime to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime2seconds(np_datetime)[source]

Convert datetime to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Parameters:

np_datetime (datetime or array-like) – Datetime instance(s).

Returns:

1D array of seconds (int64).

Return type:

ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_from_calarray(calarray, hour=12, minute=0, sec=0, ms=0)[source]

Build a datetime array from an array of calendar dates.

Parameters:

  • calarray (array-like, 1D or 2D) – Columns order: year, month, mday, hour, minute, second, microsecond.

  • hour (int, optional) – Default hour if not in calarray (default 12).

  • minute (int, optional) – Default minute (default 0).

  • sec (int, optional) – Default second (default 0).

  • ms (int, optional) – Default millisecond (default 0).

Returns:

1D array of datetime instances.

Return type:

ndarray

Notes

year, month, mday are mandatory. If hour/minute/sec/ms not provided, defaults are hour=12, minute=0, sec=0, ms=0 (middle of day).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.datetime_round_second(my_datetime)[source]

Round a Python datetime instance to the nearest second.

Parameters:

my_datetime (datetime) – Datetime instance to round.

Returns:

New rounded datetime (microsecond set to 0).

Return type:

datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.day_since_decyear(decyear, ref_decyear)[source]

Calculate the number of days since a reference decimal year.

Parameters:

  • decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year(s).

  • ref_decyear (float) – Reference decimal year.

Returns:

Days elapsed. Negative means before reference date. Useful for postseismic analysis.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> ref_decyear=pyacs.cal2decyear(16,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33))
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(17,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 32)),ref_decyear)
>>> 0.9999884259232203
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(17,4,2016,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33)),ref_decyear)
>>> 0.9999999999854481
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(pyacs.cal2decyear(16,4,2017,ut=pyacs.hmsmicros2ut(23, 58, 33)),ref_decyear)
>>> 364.9999999999345
>>> ld=pyacs.mjd2decyear(np.arange(pyacs.cal2mjd(13,4,2016),pyacs.cal2mjd(19,4,2016)))
>>> pyacs.day_since_decyear(ld,ref_decyear)
>>> array([-3.499, -2.499, -1.499, -0.499,  0.501,  1.501])
pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2cal(dayno, year)[source]

Return the day and month corresponding to day of year.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year (1-366).

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

Returns:

  • day (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2datetime(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (12:00).

Returns:

Datetime instance(s). At 12:00 by default; specify ut for other times.

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2decyear(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to decimal year.

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (12:00).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

Accounts for leap years (366 days). Unless ut is specified, the result is at 12:00 (ut=0.5).

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,1999)
>>> 1999.16301369863
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2000)
>>> 2000.1625683060108
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2001)
>>> 2001.16301369863
>>> pyacs.dayno2decyear(60,2001,ut=0.)
>>> 2001.1616438356164
pyacs.lib.astrotime.dayno2mjd(dayno, year, ut=0.5)[source]

Convert day of year and year to modified Julian day (JD - 2400000.5).

Parameters:

  • dayno (int or array-like) – Day of year.

  • year (int or array-like) – Year.

  • ut (float, optional) – Day fraction. Default 0.5 (middle of day).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

Notes

When ut is not provided, the middle of the day is used.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.dayno2mjd(60,2000)
>>> 51603.5
>>> pyacs.dayno2mjd(60.,2000,ut=0)
>>> 51603.0
pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2cal(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to calendar date.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

  • mday (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2datetime(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2dayno(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to day of year and day fraction.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

  • noday (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2epoch(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to formatted epoch string ‘yy:doy:sec’ (%02d:%03d:%05d).

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Epoch string(s) ‘yy:doy:sec’.

Return type:

str or ndarray

Notes

The input decimal year is assumed to account for leap years; the day of year is the decimal part of decyear times the number of days in the year.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.cal2decyear(1,3,2001)
>>> 2001.16301369863
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(2001.16301369863)
>>> '01:060:43200'
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(2000.16301369863)
>>> '00:060:57284'
>>> pyacs.decyear2epoch(pyacs.cal2decyear(1,3,2000))
>>> '00:061:43200'
pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2mjd(decyear)[source]

Convert decimal year to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year.

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.decyear2seconds(np_decyear, rounding='day')[source]

Convert decimal year to seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Parameters:

  • np_decyear (float or array-like) – Decimal year(s).

  • rounding ({'day', 'hour', 'minute', 'second'}, optional) – Rounding: ‘day’ at 12:00:00, ‘hour’ at 00:00, ‘minute’ at current minute with 00 seconds, ‘second’ at integer second.

Returns:

1D integer (np.int64) array of seconds.

Return type:

ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.epoch2decyear(epoch)[source]

Convert SINEX-format epoch string ‘YY:DOY:SOD’ (e.g. ‘17:100:43185’) to decimal year.

Parameters:

epoch (str or array-like) – Epoch string(s).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.gpsweek2mjd(gpsweek, dow)[source]

Convert GPS week and day of week to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

  • gpsweek (int or array-like) – GPS week number.

  • dow (int or array-like) – Day of week (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday).

Returns:

Modified Julian day.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.guess_date(date)[source]

Guess a date from input (string or float) and return decimal year.

Parameters:

date (str or float) – Date as string (e.g. ‘2016/04/16’, ‘2016 107’, ‘57500.0’) or float (MJD or decimal year).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float

Examples

Calendar date formats (year/month/day or day/month/year): >>> import pyacs >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016/04/16’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016-04-16’) 2016.2909836065573

Day of year format (year doy or doy year): >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016 107’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘107 2016’) 2016.2909836065573

Decimal year format: >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘2016.29’) 2016.29 >>> pyacs.guess_date(2016.29) 2016.29

MJD (Modified Julian Date) format: >>> pyacs.guess_date(‘57500.0’) 2016.2909836065573 >>> pyacs.guess_date(57500.0) 2016.2909836065573

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2ut(h, mn, s=0, microsecond=0)[source]

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to fractional day.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Hours.

  • mn (int) – Minutes.

  • s (int, optional) – Seconds (default 0).

  • microsecond (int or float, optional) – Microseconds (default 0).

Returns:

Fractional day (ut) in [0., 1.[.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.hmsmicros2uts(h, mn, s=0, microsecond=0)[source]

Convert hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Hours.

  • mn (int) – Minutes.

  • s (int, optional) – Seconds (default 0).

  • microsecond (int or float, optional) – Microseconds (default 0).

Returns:

Seconds since 00:00:00.0 (uts).

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jd2mjd(jd)[source]

Convert Julian day to modified Julian day.

Parameters:

jd (float) – Julian day.

Returns:

jd - 2400000.5.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_datetime(jpldate)[source]

Convert JPL date to datetime object.

JPL dates are seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 (TAI).

Parameters:

jpldate (float or array-like) – JPL date (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.jpldate_2_decyear(jpldate)[source]

Convert JPL date to decimal year.

JPL dates are seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 (TAI).

Parameters:

jpldate (float or array-like) – JPL date (seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00 TAI).

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.leap_year(year)[source]

Return True if year is a leap year.

Parameters:

year (int or array-like) – Year (YYYY).

Returns:

True if leap year, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2cal(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to calendar date (universal time).

Based on the slalib routine sla_djcl.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day (JD - 2400000.5).

Returns:

  • day (int or ndarray) – Day of month.

  • month (int or ndarray) – Month (1-12).

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction in [0., 1.[.

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2datetime(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to a Python datetime instance.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray of datetime

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2dayno(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to year and day of year (universal time).

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

  • dayno (int or ndarray) – Day of year.

  • year (int or ndarray) – Year.

  • ut (float or ndarray) – Day fraction.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.mjd2dayno(51603.0)
>>> (60, 2000, 0.0)
>>> pyacs.mjd2dayno(51603.5)
>>> (60, 2000, 0.5)
pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2decyear(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to decimal year.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

Decimal year.

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2gpsweek(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to GPS week and GPS day of week.

Parameters:

mjd (float or array-like) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

  • gps_week (int or ndarray) – GPS week number.

  • day_of_week (int or ndarray) – Day of week (0-6).

pyacs.lib.astrotime.mjd2jd(mjd)[source]

Convert modified Julian day to Julian day.

Parameters:

mjd (float) – Modified Julian day.

Returns:

mjd + 2400000.5.

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.seconds2datetime(seconds)[source]

Convert seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00 to datetime object(s).

Parameters:

seconds (int or array-like) – Seconds since 1980-01-01 00:00:00.

Returns:

Datetime instance(s).

Return type:

datetime or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.ut2uts(ut)[source]

Convert fractional day to seconds since 00:00:00.0.

Parameters:

ut (float) – Fractional day in [0., 1.[.

Returns:

ut * 60.*60.*24. (seconds in day).

Return type:

float

pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2hmsmicros(uts)[source]

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 (0-86400) to hours, minutes, seconds, microseconds.

Parameters:

uts (float or array-like) – Seconds since 00:00:00.0, in [0., 86400.[.

Returns:

  • h (int or ndarray) – Hours.

  • mn (int or ndarray) – Minutes.

  • s (int or ndarray) – Seconds.

  • microsecond (float or ndarray) – Microseconds.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.uts2hmsmicros(86399.999999)
>>> (23, 59, 59, 999999.0000069374)
>>> pyacs.uts2hmsmicros(86400)
>>> !!!  86400 uts out of range [0- 86400.0 [
pyacs.lib.astrotime.uts2ut(uts)[source]

Convert seconds since 00:00:00.0 to fractional day.

Parameters:

uts (float or array-like) – Seconds in [0., 86400.[.

Returns:

uts / (60.*60.*24.) (fractional day).

Return type:

float or ndarray

pyacs.lib.astrotime.year2yr(year)[source]

Convert 4-digit year to 2-digit year.

Parameters:

year (int or array-like) – 4-digit year (e.g. 2001).

Returns:

2-digit year (e.g. 01). year-2000 if year>1999, else year-1900.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Notes

Heritage from geodesy convention (GPS in 1980). See examples.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.year2yr(1996)
>>> 96
>>> pyacs.year2yr(2011)
>>> 11
pyacs.lib.astrotime.yr2year(yr)[source]

Convert two-digit year to four-digit year.

Parameters:

yr (int or array-like) – 2-digit year (e.g. 01).

Returns:

4-digit year (e.g. 2001). yr+2000 if yr<80, else yr+1900.

Return type:

int or ndarray

Notes

Heritage from geodesy convention (GPS era). See examples.

Examples

>>> import pyacs
>>> pyacs.yr2year(96)
>>> 1996
>>> pyacs.yr2year(11)
>>> 2011
